name: White Chili with Salsa Verde
recipeIngredient: 18 ounces Frozen Shoepeg White Corn, thawed
8 ounces Canned diced green chiles, undrained
1/2 cup Chopped fresh cilantro or parsley
1/2 teaspoon Adobo seasoning or garlic powder
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine water, lemon pepper, and cumin seed; bring to a boil.
2. Add chicken breast halves. Reduce heat to low; cover and simmer for 20-28 minutes or until chicken is fork tender and juices run clear.
3. Remove chicken from bones; cut into 1-inch pieces. Return chicken to saucepan.
4. Spray medium skillet with cooking spray; heat over medium heat. Add minced garlic; cook, stirring, for 1 minute. Remove from pan; add to chicken mixture.
5. Add onions to skillet; cook, stirring, until tender.
6. Add cooked onions, corn, chiles, ground cumin, and lime juice to chicken mixture. Bring to a boil.
7. Add beans; cook until thoroughly heated.
8. Combine all salsa ingredients in medium bowl; mix well. Refrigerate 30 minutes to blend flavors.
9. To serve, place some tortilla chips and cheese in individual soup bowls; ladle hot chili over cheese. Serve with the salsa.
